title: Choose the Right Python Testing Surface - Quick Check

questions.question: Why is pytest the default tool recommendation in this course?
questions.answer: Because it reduces friction in the TDD loop with lightweight test syntax, fixtures, parametrization, and clearer failure output.

questions.question: What does pytest often improve before a codebase is structurally migrated?
questions.answer: Execution ergonomics such as selection, tracebacks, and output clarity, even when older unittest tests still remain in place.

questions.question: What does 'migrate by pressure' mean?
questions.answer: Rewrite toward pytest-native style where it materially reduces testing friction, not because the team wants a broad rewrite for its own sake.

questions.question: Which feature does not come automatically inside `unittest.TestCase` methods just because pytest runs them?
questions.explanation: Architectural differences still block full pytest fixture arguments inside `TestCase` methods.

questions.question: What is the best first question when choosing a test surface?

options.B: Which surface makes the next useful test easiest to express?
options.C: Which tool uses the fewest imports?

questions.explanation: The course evaluates tools by how they support practical short-cycle testing work.
